commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a2dda479e4cf64e07525712b693c814cd4b46277)
commands.review_summary:
The branch snapshots and restores the selected TUI member in `loadSelectedCluster`, adds a direct reload regression test, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: select a non-first member, reload the selected cluster, and current main clears the state before preservation can read it. I did not run the interactive TUI in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs a contributor or maintainer repair for the refresh reorder case plus contributor-provided after-fix real TUI proof; automation should not be requested while the proof gate is unmet.

target.title: fix: preserve TUI member selection across cluster reload
target.body:
## Problem

In the cluster-browser TUI, the highlighted member selection silently
resets to the first row while you are reading it. It is easy to
misattribute to keyboard/scroll actions, but the real trigger is the
periodic **auto-refresh timer** (and every other reload path).

Captured with state logging — every member-index reset coincides with
`tuiAutoRefreshMsg`, never with a key:

```
19:38:03 Key(enter)         members→detail  mi=6
19:38:05 tuiAutoRefreshMsg  detail          mi 6→1   ← timer, no input
19:38:21 Key(left)          detail→members  mi=1     ← already reset
```

## Root cause

`loadSelectedCluster()` clears `memberIndex = -1` / `memberRows = nil`
*before* loading detail. `sortMembers()` then tries to preserve the
selection via `selectedMember()`, but that depends on exactly the
state `loadSelectedCluster()` just wiped, so `selectedID` is always `0`
on this path and the restore loop never matches — the selection falls
back to `firstSelectableMemberIndex()`.

The preservation logic is effectively dead on **every** reload path:
the auto-refresh timer, manual refresh (`r`), sort toggle (`s`),
member-sort, filter/min-size (`f`), `loadSelectedThreadNeighbors`,
cluster mouse-clicks, repository switch — all call
`loadSelectedCluster()` and all drop the member selection.

## Fix

Snapshot the selected member's stable `Thread.ID` at the top of
`loadSelectedCluster()` *before* the wipe, and re-apply it after the
rebuild via a small `restoreMemberSelection` helper. Threads are
disjoint across clusters (verified: 0 threads belong to >1 cluster in
both run and durable views), so an id match implies the same cluster is
still selected; when the cluster was switched or the member filtered
out, the existing first-selectable default stands. No new model state.

## Test

`TestTUILoadSelectedClusterPreservesMemberSelection` drives
`loadSelectedCluster()` with a non-first member selected and asserts it
survives a reload. It **fails on the prior behavior** (`got thread 101,
want 102`) and passes with the fix. Full `internal/cli` package and
`go vet ./...` pass.
target.author: stainlu
